steps: Identify the cost components: a fixed \$25 service fee plus \$10 per hour for \(t\) hours. Total cost = \(25 + 10t\).
The person intends to spend at most \$75, so the total cost must be less than or equal to 75: $$25 + 10t \leq 75$$

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: \(10t \leq 75\) — Omits the fixed \$25 service fee entirely. Eliminated.
  • B: \(10 + 25t \leq 75\) — Swaps the service fee and hourly rate (\$10 fixed, \$25/hr), contradicting the problem. Eliminated.
  • C: \(25t \leq 75\) — Uses only the service fee amount as the hourly rate and drops the fixed fee structure. Eliminated.
  • D: \(25 + 10t \leq 75\) — Correctly reflects the \$25 fixed fee plus \$10 per hour not exceeding \$75. That matches choice D — D is correct.
